title: 3D 1v1 FPS Battle Game with Weapons and AI Difficulty

requirements.summary: Create a new React Three Fiber / Three.js game hook `use1v1Lol3DGame.ts` that implements a 3D first-person/third-person 1v1 battle game with player state, AI bot state, projectile physics, and collision detection in a 3D arena
acceptanceCriteria: Hook exports game state (player, bot, projectiles, gamePhase, score) and action handlers
Player and bot each have HP that decreases when hit by projectiles
Game phase transitions: idle → playing → game-over
All state updates occur inside a requestAnimationFrame game loop

file_operations.description: Create a new React hook that manages 3D game state for a 1v1 FPS battle. The hook must export game state (player position/HP/weapon/ammo, bot position/HP/weapon/ammo/difficulty, projectiles array, gamePhase enum, score object) and action handlers (startGame, fire, switchWeapon, movePlayer, selectDifficulty, reset). Implement a ref-based requestAnimationFrame game loop that updates projectile positions, checks 3D collision detection between projectiles and player/bot, decrements HP on hits, and transitions gamePhase from idle → playing → game-over. Player and bot each start with 100 HP. Track all state in refs for performance and expose read-only state via useState for React rendering.

requirements.summary: Implement four weapons (Sniper, Shotgun, Pistol, Machine Gun) with distinct stats, switchable via number keys 1–4, each tracking ammo and reload state
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing keys 1, 2, 3, 4 switches to Sniper, Shotgun, Pistol, Machine Gun respectively
Each weapon has correct ammo capacity and reload timer
Firing is blocked when ammo is 0 and weapon is reloading
Shotgun fires multiple pellets in a spread pattern
HUD displays current weapon name, ammo count, and reload progress

file_operations.description: Add weapon system with four weapon types: Sniper (1 ammo, 2.5s reload, 80 damage), Shotgun (5 ammo, 1.5s reload, 6 pellets × 15 damage each, spread pattern), Pistol (20 ammo, 0.8s reload, 25 damage), Machine Gun (25 ammo, 1.2s reload, 12 damage, rapid fire). Track currentWeapon, ammo, reloadTimer, and isReloading for both player and bot. Implement switchWeapon handler that responds to keys 1–4. Block firing when ammo is 0 or isReloading is true. For Shotgun, generate 6 projectiles with randomized spread angles. Automatically trigger reload when ammo reaches 0. Expose weapon state in the hook's return value.

requirements.summary: Implement four AI difficulty levels (Easy, Medium, Hard, Extreme) that scale by reaction time and accuracy, with dynamic weapon switching based on distance to player and a pre-game difficulty selection screen
acceptanceCriteria: Difficulty selection screen appears before the game starts with Easy/Medium/Hard/Extreme options
AI reaction time matches the specified delay per difficulty
AI aim accuracy scales with difficulty (higher difficulty = tighter shot spread)
AI switches to Sniper when player is far, Shotgun when close, Pistol/MachineGun at medium range
AI reloads weapons when empty just like the player

file_operations.description: Add AI difficulty system with four levels: Easy (1500ms reaction, 40% accuracy), Medium (800ms reaction, 60% accuracy), Hard (350ms reaction, 80% accuracy), Extreme (80ms reaction, 95% accuracy). Store selectedDifficulty in state and expose selectDifficulty handler. In the game loop, implement AI logic that calculates distance to player and switches weapons (Sniper if distance > 30 units, Shotgun if distance < 10 units, Pistol/MachineGun otherwise, with thresholds scaled by difficulty). Apply reaction delay before AI fires. Add random aim offset inversely proportional to accuracy percentage. Ensure AI reloads when ammo is 0. Start gamePhase in 'idle' to allow difficulty selection before transitioning to 'playing'.

requirements.summary: Build a 3D arena scene in `OneLolGame.tsx` using React Three Fiber with ground, walls, cover obstacles, pointer-lock WASD movement, visible AI bot mesh, and projectile rendering
acceptanceCriteria: Arena renders in 3D with ground, walls, and at least 6 cover obstacles
Player can move with WASD and look with mouse (pointer lock)
AI bot is represented by a 3D mesh (e.g., box or capsule) that moves toward/away from player
Projectiles are visible as small 3D meshes traveling from shooter to target
Game renders at 60fps with no major visual glitches

file_operations.description: Replace the existing 2D canvas-based 1v1 game with a 3D React Three Fiber implementation. Import Canvas from @react-three/fiber and PointerLockControls from @react-three/drei. Replace the use1v1LolGame hook with use1v1Lol3DGame. Render a <Canvas> element with a 3D arena: a flat ground plane (BoxGeometry scaled wide and thin), perimeter walls (4 tall BoxGeometry boxes forming a square boundary), and at least 6 cover obstacles (BoxGeometry boxes or cylinders positioned around the arena). Add <PointerLockControls> and implement WASD movement in a useFrame hook that updates player position in the game hook. Render the AI bot as a colored BoxGeometry or CapsuleGeometry mesh at bot.position. Map projectiles array to small SphereGeometry or CylinderGeometry meshes positioned at each projectile's 3D coordinates. Add ambient and directional lights. Remove all canvas-based rendering code.

requirements.summary: Add an in-game HUD overlaid on the 3D canvas showing player HP, AI HP, current weapon/ammo, reload progress, difficulty label, and a centered crosshair
acceptanceCriteria: Player HP bar and AI HP bar are both visible during gameplay
Weapon name and ammo count update immediately on switching or firing
Reload progress bar appears and fills during reload, disappears when complete
Crosshair is centered on screen at all times during gameplay
Difficulty label is visible during the match

file_operations.description: Add a HUD overlay positioned absolutely over the 3D Canvas. Include: a player HP bar (top-left, styled with neon green), an AI HP bar (top-right, styled with neon red), a weapon/ammo display (bottom-center, showing currentWeapon name and ammo count like 'Pistol 18/20'), a reload progress bar (below weapon display, visible only when isReloading is true, filling based on reloadTimer progress), a difficulty label (top-center, showing selectedDifficulty), and a crosshair (fixed center of screen, small cross or dot). Use Tailwind classes for positioning and neon arcade styling. Ensure HUD elements do not interfere with pointer-lock controls.

requirements.summary: Show a game-over screen when either player or AI reaches 0 HP, displaying winner, match result, and a 'Play Again' button that resets to difficulty selection; integrate ScoreSubmission on player win
acceptanceCriteria: Game-over screen appears immediately when HP reaches 0
Screen clearly states 'You Win' or 'You Lose'
'Play Again' button returns to the difficulty selection screen and resets all state
Score submission form appears on win screen
Leaderboard is shown below score submission on win

file_operations.description: Add conditional rendering for gamePhase. When gamePhase is 'game-over', hide the Canvas and HUD, and show a game-over overlay. Display 'You Win!' if player HP > 0 and bot HP is 0, or 'You Lose!' if bot HP > 0 and player HP is 0. Show the final score. If player won, render the existing ScoreSubmission component (pass score as prop) and Leaderboard component below it (use gameName='1v1-lol-3d', preferHigher=true). Include a 'Play Again' button that calls the reset handler from use1v1Lol3DGame, which should reset gamePhase to 'idle' (returning to difficulty selection), reset HP, ammo, and all game state. Ensure difficulty selection screen is shown when gamePhase is 'idle'.
